description: Evaluate the integration of residential and employment activities in San Francisco by identifying 'Vertical Mixed-Use' (VMU) buildings and aggregating to the zone level. Using Jupyter Lab and the UrbanSim datastore at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, perform a data analysis in the notebook /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/mixed_use_analysis.ipynb. Identify VMU buildings as those containing strictly greater than 0 households and greater than 0 jobs. Aggregate building data to the zone level (zone_id) to compute total_buildings, vmu_buildings, pct_vmu, vmu_households, and vmu_jobs. Filter the results to include only zones with at least 10 total buildings. Export the filtered zone-level dataframe to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/vmu_by_zone.csv. Save citywide summary statistics to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/vmu_summary.json with the keys: 'total_analyzed_zones', 'citywide_vmu_buildings', 'citywide_pct_vmu', and 'top_vmu_zone_id'. Generate a scatter plot of 'total_buildings' (X-axis) versus 'pct_vmu' (Y-axis) for the filtered zones and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/vmu_scatter.png. The notebook must be fully executed and saved.
